caption: Brisket's been on Old Faithful since 4 a.m. Low and slow, no shortcuts.
Finished another birdhouse for the back fence. Cedar this time. Wrens already had a look.
Wolverines took both games Saturday. Proud of these kids.
Collards coming in strong this year. Neighbors already asking.
Ribs, beans, and slaw plate for the Sunday cookout. Swipe for the spread.
Sanded down a little cutting board for the kitchen. Knees ache, but the shop's my happy place.
Wolverines practice gallery. Swipe through. These kids put in work.
Smoked a couple chickens for the church potluck. Recipe stays in the family, sorry folks.
Slow-motion of the bark forming on the brisket. Could watch this all day.
Lions game on, smoker going. Can't ask for much more.
Built a new planter box for the collards. Step-by-step in the carousel.
Wolverines practice in the rain today. These kids don't quit, and neither do I.
Old Faithful's been good to me. Brisket and a couple racks for the family cookout.
Quick walkthrough of the new birdhouse build. Slanted roof so the rain runs off.
Tigers opening day. Been waiting all winter for this one.
Cornbread and collard greens kind of evening. Garden's treating us right.
First frost coming. Pulled the last of the peppers tonight.
Wolverines end-of-season cookout. Brisket for the families, trophies for the kids. Whole album here.
Gospel choir rehearsal ran long but it was worth it. Sunday's going to be something.
Re-staining the birdhouses out back before winter sets in. Little touch-up.
Saturday cook for the whole block. Brisket, ribs, chicken, the works. Swipe.
Tomatoes finally turning red. Patience pays off in the garden.
Sunday service was a blessing. Sharing a little of the choir from this morning.
New cedar birdhouse with a copper roof. My best one yet, if I do say so.
Brisket cracked on the smoker at 4am, Old Faithful earning its name
First game of the season for the Wolverines, let's go girls
Sunday morning at Greater Hope, grateful
Pre-shift coffee, Detroit sunrise, and a devotional
New birdhouse for the front porch, cedar shingles
Ruth's collards recipe, Denise says I need more vinegar
Union brothers and sisters at Local 247, solidarity
Wolverines practice under the lights at Miller Park
Marcus back home for winter break, kid grew again
Old Faithful loaded with ribs for the Labor Day cookout
First frame off the router table this week, walnut and maple
Deacon board dinner at Pastor's house, blessed with good company
The tomatoes finally came in, two weeks late but sweet
Anniversary 29 with Denise, Charleston next year promise
Wolverines got their first W of the season
Bible study on Ephesians tonight, iron sharpens iron
Ruth's porch is fixed, only took three Saturdays
Detroit skyline from Belle Isle, home
Old Faithful smoking overnight for the church picnic tomorrow
Dominoes night at Big D's, Pete brought sweet tea
Fresh mow on Ruth's lawn, grandbaby helped
Chassis line before shift, 30 years and counting
New shelf for Denise's canning jars, solid oak
Sunday dinner at the Dawson house, Denise did the ribs
Wolverines banquet planning, Nov 14 mark your calendars
Sunset on Cheboygan lake, annual fishing trip with Darnell
Church van repair complete, Linda thanked me twice
Old Faithful hitting 225 like clockwork, brisket at 4am
Marcus texted, made the dean's list this semester
Rebuilt the smoker's firebox this weekend, better airflow
Union hall packed for the membership meeting
Christmas Eve service tomorrow, anchoring the ushering team
Late night in the workshop finishing Ruth's medicine cabinet
Coaching from the third base box, brace on, knee holding
Winter reading pile, MLK, Coleman Young, Detroit history
New bench for the front porch, Denise picked the stain
Practice under the March lights, season opener in three weeks
Home from the plant, still on cloud nine after that grievance win
Steel-toed Sundays after service, yardwork calling
Old Faithful competition entry, brisket ribs chicken
Testing a new dry rub, coffee grounds and brown sugar
Wolverines pizza party after the win, coach approves
Big D and I built a fire pit for his backyard
Ruth's front step, pressure-treated 4x4s all level
Frame job for Denise's Crestwood classroom, walnut and maple
The season closer at Miller Park, what a group of kids
Downtown Detroit murals with Marcus this weekend
Late night notebook entry, gratitude list
Old Faithful getting a new grate, time earned
Foreman brought coffee this morning, small kindness
Union brothers back in town from Region 1A meeting
The Wolverines new practice uniforms came in
Marcus home for the break, brought his roommate
Frost on the pumpkins, winter's near
Sunday morning choir sounded like heaven itself
Deacon Terry at your service, ushering team briefing done
Ushering team briefing at 8am sharp, coffee provided
Boots-up before the shift horn, chassis line ready
New notebook for the season, journals 41
Denise's tomato bed is the envy of Greenview Street
Old Faithful competition placed 2nd, not bad for a backyard
Wolverines first win in the tournament bracket
Ruth's Sunday chicken still my favorite meal
Grievance filed for a good brother, this one's personal
First snow on the Old Faithful cover, winter's here
Season opener flag ceremony at Miller Park
Union hall shot with Carla and Ray, solidarity
Deacon meeting at Greater Hope, budget review night
Frame for Denise's classroom map, quartersawn white oak
Detroit Tigers game with Marcus, he caught a foul
Post-shift sunset over the plant, twenty-eight years counting
Wolverines end-of-season banquet Nov 14 save the date
Dominoes rematch, Big D wins by two points
Church picnic prep in full swing, Old Faithful ready
Rebuilt the workbench in the garage, solid maple top
Pastor Caldwell preached today like it was his last sermon
Marcus home for winter break, hitting the weight room
